message:
refactor(quality): Tune mobile quality settings for smoother 60fps target

Adjusts the quality tier settings to better balance visual fidelity
with performance across device capabilities:

Low Tier Changes:
- Reduced particleMultiplier from 0.25 to 0.2 (fewer particles)
- Reduced maxParticles from 200 to 180
- Increased targetFps from 30 to 60 (smoother animations)

Medium Tier Changes:
- Reduced particleMultiplier from 0.5 to 0.45
- Reduced geometryDetail from 0.75 to 0.7
- Increased targetFps from 30 to 60 (smoother animations)

The philosophy shift here is: rather than running at 30fps with more
particles, run at 60fps with slightly fewer particles. Users perceive
smooth motion as higher quality than dense but choppy visuals. This
aligns with modern display refresh rates (60Hz+ being standard).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -145,23 +145,23 @@ function getQualitySettings(caps: DeviceCapabilities): QualitySettings {
   switch (caps.tier) {
     case "low":
       return {
-        particleMultiplier: 0.25,
+        particleMultiplier: 0.2,
         geometryDetail: 0.5,
         maxDpr: 1,
         enableShadows: false,
         enablePostProcessing: false,
-        maxParticles: 200,
-        targetFps: 30,
+        maxParticles: 180,
+        targetFps: 60,
       };
     case "medium":
       return {
-        particleMultiplier: 0.5,
-        geometryDetail: 0.75,
+        particleMultiplier: 0.45,
+        geometryDetail: 0.7,
         maxDpr: 1.5,
         enableShadows: false,
         enablePostProcessing: false,
         maxParticles: 500,
-        targetFps: 30,
+        targetFps: 60,
       };
     case "high":
     default:
